items.title: God and Man at MIT
items.summary: Examines the intersection of faith and academic life at MIT, exploring how Catholic intellectuals navigate secular higher education environments.
items.ggiConnection: Directly relevant to GGI's mission of Catholic intellectual formation and the integration of faith with rigorous academic inquiry.

items.title: The Word Became Flesh and Picked Up a Hammer
items.summary: Reflects on the dignity of manual labor and craftsmanship through a theological lens, connecting the Incarnation to the value of physical work.
items.ggiConnection: Aligns with GGI's holistic approach to formation and the integration of contemplation with practical skills and craftsmanship.

items.title: Ratzinger in the Whirlwind
items.summary: Analysis of Pope Benedict XVI (Ratzinger) during turbulent times in the Church, exploring his theological contributions and leadership during crisis.
items.ggiConnection: Connects to GGI's focus on the Catholic intellectual tradition and proper understanding of Church teaching and leadership.

items.title: “Ecclesiacide,” Then and Now
items.summary: Historical and contemporary analysis of attacks on the Church ('ecclesiacide'), drawing parallels between past and present challenges to Catholic institutional life.
items.ggiConnection: Aligns with GGI's concerns about cultural renewal and defending Catholic institutions and traditions.

items.title: Declinism Is Easy: Art, Beauty, and the Need to Make it New
items.summary: Thoughtful critique of cultural pessimism, arguing for the importance of art and beauty in cultural renewal rather than simply lamenting decline.
items.ggiConnection: Directly connects to GGI's emphasis on beauty, cultural renewal, and the positive work of creating rather than merely critiquing.

items.title: Seeing Woman through the Eyes of Gertrud von le Fort
items.summary: Intellectual analysis of German Catholic writer Gertrud von le Fort's theological anthropology of women, offering substantive engagement with Catholic literary tradition.
items.ggiConnection: Strong connection to Catholic intellectual tradition, Great Books, and literary analysis

items.title: Forming Families to Sing With Angels
items.summary: Explores liturgical music education for families, emphasizing the formation of children in sacred song as part of domestic church life.
items.ggiConnection: Strong connection to liturgy, beauty, and family formation themes central to GGI mission

items.title: Against Concelebration: The Remarkable Intervention of Archbishop Paul-Pierre Philippe OP at Vatican II
items.summary: Historical examination of Archbishop Philippe's theological arguments against widespread concelebration during Vatican II debates.
items.ggiConnection: Substantial intellectual content on liturgical theology that aligns with GGI's Catholic intellectual tradition and liturgical formation.

items.title: What Can Be Done about the Universities?
items.summary: Addresses the crisis in higher education with potential solutions, likely focusing on intellectual integrity and educational reform. Critical topic for Canadian educational renewal.
items.ggiConnection: Directly relevant to GGI's educational mission and formation work

items.title: The Need for Non-Ironic Limits: A Review of The Philosophy of Philip Rieff
items.summary: Reviews Philip Rieff's philosophy examining the need for cultural limits and authority structures against therapeutic individualism.
items.ggiConnection: Strong connection to GGI themes of cultural renewal and the Catholic intellectual tradition's engagement with secular philosophy and cultural criticism.

items.title: The Evangelical Power of the Old Testament
items.summary: Bishop Barron explores how the Old Testament serves as a powerful evangelical tool, demonstrating its continued relevance for contemporary Catholic evangelization.
items.ggiConnection: Connects to GGI's emphasis on Scripture study and the Catholic intellectual tradition of biblical interpretation.

items.title: How To Truly KNOW Something | Dr. Trent Dougherty
items.summary: Philosophical discussion with Dr. Trent Dougherty on epistemology and the nature of true knowledge from a Catholic perspective.
items.ggiConnection: Strong alignment with GGI's faith and reason integration, philosophical education, and Catholic intellectual tradition.

items.title: We Receive God's Own Life | Contemplata Ep 52
items.summary: Episode exploring the concept of receiving divine life through grace, examining the theological foundations of supernatural participation in God's nature.
items.ggiConnection: Strongly aligns with GGI's Thomistic approach to theology and emphasis on the transformative power of Catholic intellectual formation.

items.title: How Science Killed Materialism
items.summary: Explores how modern scientific discoveries challenge materialist worldviews, arguing that empirical evidence increasingly points beyond purely material explanations of reality.
items.ggiConnection: Aligns with GGI's faith and reason integration, addressing the relationship between science and Catholic intellectual tradition.

items.title: America’s Prodigal Fathers (ft. Timothy Goeglein)
items.summary: Features Timothy Goeglein discussing the crisis of absent fathers in American culture and its broader societal implications for family formation.
items.ggiConnection: Connects to GGI's emphasis on community formation and cultural renewal through strengthened family structures.

items.title: When Envy Turns Apocalyptic
items.summary: Analyzes how envy as a vice can escalate into apocalyptic thinking and destructive social movements, drawing on classical moral theology.
items.ggiConnection: Engages with classical virtue ethics tradition central to GGI's Great Books and Catholic intellectual heritage.

items.title: The West Distorted
items.summary: Examination of how Western civilization has been misrepresented or misunderstood, likely addressing cultural decline and the need for authentic understanding of our heritage.
items.ggiConnection: Directly aligns with GGI's mission of cultural renewal and proper understanding of Western Christian civilization.

items.title: Poetry as Gift: Eucharistic Miracle
items.summary: Exploration of poetry as a form of gift and its connection to Eucharistic spirituality, examining the intersection of literary art and sacramental theology.
items.ggiConnection: Connects to GGI's focus on beauty, liturgy, and the integration of arts with Catholic spiritual life.

items.title: Thinking Christianly About Technology Again: Technology and the Fragmentation of Society
items.summary: Christian analysis of technology's impact on social cohesion and community life, offering theological perspective on contemporary digital challenges.
items.ggiConnection: Connects to GGI's interest in cultural renewal and community formation in the face of modern challenges.

items.title: What to do with the conversion boom?
items.summary: Substantive analysis of how the Catholic Church should respond to increasing conversion rates and what pastoral strategies are needed.
items.ggiConnection: Strongly connects to GGI's mission of formation and community building in Catholic cultural renewal
